name: GAF_GenAI_ValidateSpec_Tool_v1
description: Validate a tool or agent specification against naming conventions and portability rules.
instructions: Use this tool to validate a specification BEFORE creating or updating an asset. Catches errors early. Validation rules: 1. Naming - Must follow GAF_<Product>_<Capability>_(Tool|Agent)_v<N> format. 2. Portability - No hardcoded tenant URLs or environment identifiers. 3. Required fields - name, instructions, type, data must be present. 4. Type-specific - TOOLKIT type requires utterances array. Parameters: spec (required) - The complete specification object to validate. Returns: valid (true if spec passes all validation), errors (Array of {field, message} objects if validation fails). Always call this before GAF_GenAI_CreateTool_Tool_v1, GAF_GenAI_CreateAgent_Tool_v1, or GAF_GenAI_UpdateAsset_Tool_v1.

data.api_docs:
This is a client-side validation tool that checks specifications against Factory conventions. No API call is made - validation is performed locally. Validation checks performed: 1. GAF naming convention: GAF_<Product>_<Capability>_(Tool|Agent)_v<N>. 2. Required fields present: name, instructions, type, data. 3. No hardcoded tenant URLs (inforcloudsuite.com, mingledev.com). 4. No hardcoded tenant IDs. 5. Type-specific requirements (TOOLKIT needs utterances). 6. api_docs must contain Example Request section. 7. responseInstructions must contain debug template.
Example Request:
Validate spec object (client-side, no HTTP call made)
data.responseInstructions:
Returns a validation result object. Success response: {"valid":true,"errors":[]}. Failure response: {"valid":false,"errors":[{"field":"name","message":"Invalid name format..."}]}.

CRITICAL - IF ERROR OCCURS INCLUDE THE BELOW IN RESPONSE:
---DEBUG---
FULL_URL: [The complete URL that was called]
FULL_PAYLOAD: [The exact request body sent (JSON format)]
FULL_RESPONSE: [The complete API response received]
---END DEBUG---
